pages.text: One sunny afternoon, <name> decided he wanted to help his mom make curry. He tied a big apron around himself—it almost tripped him—and declared, “Today, I’ll be the master chef!”
Mom handed him a wooden spoon. “Stir the curry while I chop the veggies,” she said. The curry smelled spicy and delicious, bubbling happily on the stove.
<name> peered at the counter. “Salt makes curry tasty!” he thought. He grabbed a jar and poured a big spoonful into the pot. Suddenly, the curry smelled… unusual. Uh-oh! It wasn’t salt—it was sugar!

Mom stirred and tasted. Her eyes widened. “<name>! Why is it sweet?” <name> shrugged, trying to look serious. “I was just… experimenting!” The neighbors peeked through the window. “Mmm, smells like dessert!” Auntie Meena exclaimed. “Is it halwa?”
Just then, their naughty cat, Puchi, jumped onto the counter and knocked over a small jar of chili powder. ACHOO! The spices flew into the pot. Everyone coughed and laughed.

<name> tried adding more tomatoes and onions, but the curry now looked extra messy. Mom grinned. “It’s a bit sweet, a bit spicy, and a lot funny. Let’s eat it anyway!” They served the curry, everyone laughed at the sweet-spicy flavors, and even Puchi licked a little from the pot. <name> giggled. “I guess my curry experiment worked… just not the way I planned!”
